reasoning: The Lean formalization faithfully captures Munkres's exercise. The informal claim "family of topologies $\mathcal{T}_\alpha$" is encoded as `T : I → Set (Set X)` with `∀ i, is_topology X (T i)`, where `I : Type*` plays the role of the index set $\alpha$. The conclusion `is_topology X (⋂ i : I, T i)` matches "the intersection is a topology on X". The `is_topology` predicate (univ ∈ T, closed under binary intersection, closed under arbitrary union) is equivalent to the standard three topology axioms, since the empty union equals ∅, giving ∅ ∈ T automatically. The edge case of `I` empty gives the full power set, which is indeed a topology, so no hidden non-emptiness assumption is needed. Quantifier structure, types, and hypotheses all align with the informal statement.
